title: Richer InputSender implementations as needed
description: Evaluate if any agent types need non-PTY input methods. If so, implement API-based InputSender (e.g. for future Pi Agent with sendMessage). Wire InputSender selection into adapter PrepareForLaunch or NewAdapter.

comments.author: coder-2-fog
comments.text:
EVALUATION (eval-first, concierge-approved lean deliverable — document seam, do not wire).

FINDING: No current agent type needs non-PTY input. The harness registry has exactly 3 types — claude_code, codex, generic (harness.go ValidHarnessTypes) — all interactive CLIs driven through a PTY; input is delivered by writing to the PTY master. No Pi/non-PTY agent exists (the only sendMessage in-repo is the Telegram bridge).

STATE OF THE SEAM: InputSender + PTYInputSender (internal/session/agent/harness/harness.go) are DEAD CODE — zero non-test callers. They were moved 'unchanged' from the legacy adapter/ package during the harness refactor (docs/plan-agent-harness-refactor.md:423) and never wired. The LIVE input path is session/message.RunDelivery -> DeliveryConfig.PtyWriter (io.Writer) + SignalInterrupt (func), in internal/session/message/delivery.go:138-180, bypassing InputSender entirely.

TWO GAPS a real wiring must close (so this isn't a byte-for-byte swap):
1. deliver() is TUI-shaped: 0x03 Ctrl+C, type-text-then-\r with a 50ms UI-settle delay, inline-vs-file-reference by body length. An API sendMessage(text) is one atomic call — those mechanics belong behind SendInput.
2. The live interrupt is TWO parts: raw 0x03 to the PTY AND harness.HandleInterrupt() via session.SignalInterrupt (session.go). PTYInputSender.SendInterrupt only does the 0x03, so the interface's interrupt contract must grow.

RECOMMENDATION (approved): don't wire speculatively — no consumer to validate against, and deliver() is the live-fleet input hot path. Wire when a real non-PTY agent (Pi) lands, against its actual API semantics. Deliverable: annotated the InputSender doc-comment (harness.go) recording the finding + the exact future wiring point + the two gaps. Comment-only, builds/vets clean. Ready for review.
